brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is the statistics of primordial density fields (Gaussian vs chi^2_1 non-Gaussianity, early dark energy) feeding halo/cluster abundances and S-Z power spectra, number counts and correlation functions; Paper B's machinery is a differential-geometric variational problem, reducing a d-brane in light-cone gauge to a Chaplygin gas whose static equation is the vanishing of extrinsic mean curvature (the Plateau minimal-surface problem plus Hamiltonian-BRST). The only overlap is topical vocabulary ('dark energy' / Chaplygin gas appearing in cosmology), not any shared governing process, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
